title: Tethered EEG Recordings in Syngap1 rats

funding_references.funder_name: The work performed here was funded by The Simons Initiative for the Developing Brain.
Simons Initiative for the Developing Brain

description: This dataset comprises 6-hour tethered EEG recordings from wildtype and Syngap1 heterozygous mutant rats (male, 12-16 weeks old) acquired during the light phase of the circadian cycle. Recordings were obtained using a 32-channel head-mounted EEG array with integrated accelerometers at 1 kHz sampling rate. The dataset supports investigation of electrophysiological alterations associated with SYNGAP1-related pathophysiology in a rodent model.
methods_description: EEG recordings were acquired using an OpenEphys acquisition system with a head-mounted 32-channel EEG array probe (H32-EEG, NeuroNexus) equipped with accelerometers, sampled at 1 kHz. Recordings were conducted in male rats aged 12-16 weeks during the light phase (zeitgeber time 3-9 hours) under a 12-hour light/dark cycle. Most animals underwent two 6-hour recording sessions.
